title: Attach an 'error' listener to the spawned Pi child before the cleanup.ready() await window
evidence: On POSIX, spawnProcessTree returns the raw spawn() child with no 'error' listener; runPiChild only attaches `child.on("error", ...)` at line 767 inside the result Promise executor, after `await cleanup.ready()` (line 625) and the abort-listener block. An async spawn failure (EACCES/EMFILE/EAGAIN) is emitted via process.nextTick, which runs ahead of the pending promise continuations, so the 'error' event can fire with zero listeners — an unhandled 'error' on an EventEmitter throws and crashes the parent Pi extension host instead of degrading via childFailure(). WindowsContainedProcess has the same window: supervisor errors are re-emitted on the facade before the runner listener exists.
impact: Rare spawn-time failures escalate from a graceful degraded ChildResult to an uncaught exception in the host process.
recommendation: Attach a no-op/deferred 'error' handler to the child immediately after spawnProcessTree returns (or inside spawnProcessTree itself) and route any pre-attach error into the startup_failure path.
acceptance_criteria: No window exists between spawn and runner handler attachment in which a child 'error' event is unhandled
